text:
Cost assessment methods and assumptions regarding road accident costs in the European Union The assessment of road traffic accident costs is a mandatory element of any road safety strategy according to the EU directives. Due to huge differences in the amount and quality of available statistical data for assessing road accident costs across the European Union (EU), the depth and scope of road accident cost assessments also differ. As a result, road accident cost assessment values are among the most divergent economic figures and therefore are not suitable for reliable direct cross-country comparisons.

While focusing on assessing the humanistic impact of road accidents, the purpose of the study is to investigate the quantification of human losses in monetary terms and to examine the procedures used to assess accident damage in various contexts. By using the present data situation as a basis, the study clarifies the basic problems and focal points which must be taken into account when assessing the basic cost categories and when dealing with the more difficult valuation of road accidents in the social context. Special attention is given to the difficult and highly controversial question of evaluating the statistical value of human life.
